oral.description: The leaders of the practice are formally or informally identified.
oral.guideline: Where a practice consists of more than two practitioners the leaders of the practice must be formally identified.
oral.guidelines: Where a practice consists of more than two practitioners the leaders of the practice must be formally identified.

oral.description: The practice has a mission statement that reflects its strategic objectives and matches the needs of the community served.
oral.guideline: The mission statement is critical to the strategic planning process since it provides clear, guiding principles that define who the healthcare provider is as an organisation and why the practice exists. Mission statements create the foundation for action planning and a basis for accountability with the community.
oral.guidelines: The mission statement is critical to the strategic planning process since it provides clear, guiding principles that define who the healthcare provider is as an organisation and why the practice exists. Mission statements create the foundation for action planning and a basis for accountability with the community.

oral.description: The leaders are collectively responsible for ensuring that the mission statement is known to all personnel, patients, carers and the community served.
oral.guideline: This can be achieved in many ways, such as, by public display of the mission statement on notice boards, in the waiting area or included in printed information leaflets given to each patient when they first visit the practice. If the practice has a website, the mission statement can also be published on the website. Documented evidence that personnel have read the mission statement must be provided for full compliance with this criterion.
oral.guidelines: This can be achieved in many ways, such as, by public display of the mission statement on notice boards, in the waiting area or included in printed information leaflets given to each patient when they first visit the practice. If the practice has a website, the mission statement can also be published on the website. Documented evidence that personnel have read the mission statement must be provided for full compliance with this criterion.

oral.description: The practice participates in community health activities.
oral.guideline: The mission of a practice should meet the needs of the community. To be compliant with this criterion evidence must be provided of interaction with members of the health committees and participation in community health activities
oral.guidelines: The mission of a practice should meet the needs of the community. To be compliant with this criterion evidence must be provided of interaction with members of the health committees and participation in community health activities

oral.description: The leaders, in liaison with the whole practice team, coordinates the compilation of an annual strategic plan and budget.
oral.guideline: This criterion requires all departments within the practice to be involved in the compilation of the annual strategic plan and budget to ensure that the needs of each department are discussed, considered and met if possible within the scope of the practice budget. Compliance will be demonstrated by documented evidence, for example, minutes of meetings.
oral.guidelines: This criterion requires all departments within the practice to be involved in the compilation of the annual strategic plan and budget to ensure that the needs of each department are discussed, considered and met if possible within the scope of the practice budget. Compliance will be demonstrated by documented evidence, for example, minutes of meetings.

oral.description: The leaders are collectively responsible for implementing the practice's mission and strategic plan.
oral.guideline: Evidence of an action plan for achieving the practice’s mission and strategic plan will be required.
oral.guidelines: Evidence of an action plan for achieving the practice’s mission and strategic plan will be required.

oral.description: The strategic plan is evaluated on an annual basis.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured by providing documented evidence that the service's strategic plan has been evaluated to ensure that it remains reflective of the current needs of the patient population.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by providing documented evidence that the service's strategic plan has been evaluated to ensure that it remains reflective of the current needs of the patient population.

oral.description: Regular monitoring of the implementation of the strategic plan against envisaged timeframes and progress in achieving its objectives is documented at intervals determined by the practice.
oral.guideline: This criterion requires that practice leaders adhere to the timeframes that were set at the beginning of each year for achieving the strategic plan. For any plan to be implemented effectively, it is essential to monitor the progress that is being made towards achieving the outcomes that have been planned. This criterion further requires the practice to document the progress that is being made during the year towards achieving the strategic plan.
oral.guidelines: This criterion requires that practice leaders adhere to the timeframes that were set at the beginning of each year for achieving the strategic plan. For any plan to be implemented effectively, it is essential to monitor the progress that is being made towards achieving the outcomes that have been planned. This criterion further requires the practice to document the progress that is being made during the year towards achieving the strategic plan.

oral.description: Where appropriate, the leadership roles in various positions are documented, agreed to and known by the personnel.
oral.guideline: This may, for example include personnel responsible for monitoring infection control practices, resuscitation, stock control, patient files etc.
oral.guidelines: This may, for example include personnel responsible for monitoring infection control practices, resuscitation, stock control, patient files etc.

oral.description: The manager is responsible for the day-to-day running of the practice.
oral.guideline: This criterion is assessed on evidence of effective management found throughout the practice during the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC, the transgressions need to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC/NC rating and it should be based on accurate facts.
oral.guidelines: This criterion is assessed on evidence of effective management found throughout the practice during the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC, the transgressions need to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC/NC rating and it should be based on accurate facts.

oral.description: The manager has the education and/or experience necessary to carry out his or her responsibilities.
oral.guideline: If the practice manager is a solo practitioner or partner in the practice this criterion will be scored based on the evidence of effective management found throughout the practice during the survey. If the practice manager is an employee of the practice, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.
oral.guidelines: If the practice manager is a solo practitioner or partner in the practice this criterion will be scored based on the evidence of effective management found throughout the practice during the survey. If the practice manager is an employee of the practice, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.

oral.description: The practice is licensed in terms of relevant legislation for the level and type of services provided.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL Documented evidence is required.
oral.guidelines: CRITICAL Documented evidence is required.

oral.description: The manager ensures that there is a system in place to monitor the implementation of applicable laws and regulations.
oral.guideline: This criterion will be scored compliant by default, although a PC rating is given whenever there is definite evidence of non-adherence to any legal requirement. In these cases, the transgression needs to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC rating and it should be based on accurate facts. Common examples of non-conformance with legal requirements include the following deficiencies: The required certificates are not available, for example, fire clearance certificates, electrical installation certificates, commissioning certificates (for example, ethylene oxide sterilisers), pressure test certificates for vessels under pressure Internal/external financial audits are not conducted Nurses transcribe doctors' prescriptions and/or dispense medication Pharmaceutical items are incorrectly labelled Proof of current registration of professional personnel with the relevant councils is not available In order to demonstrate compliance, practices may wish to compile a template listing the criteria requiring demonstration of compliance with country-specific legislation and regulations against the relevant national acts, regulations, etc. applicable to the criterion.
oral.guidelines: This criterion will be scored compliant by default, although a PC rating is given whenever there is definite evidence of non-adherence to any legal requirement. oral.description: There is evidence of response to any reports from inspecting and regulatory authorities.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL  This requires documented evidence for whatever inspections may have been conducted. Examples may include inspections by the following authorities: The local fire authority for fire clearance purposes The national authority responsible for radiation safety aspects The national authority responsible for health and safety compliance The national authority responsible for financial compliance The national authority responsible for maintenance requirements such as boiler inspections and pressure tests, autoclave pressure tests The local authority responsible for testing of water supplies The relevant professional bodies where student training is provided Licensing authorities for facilities such as the pharmacy, etc. Licensing authorities for facilities. Immigration requirements for non-resident

oral.description: The manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.
oral.guideline: The criterion will be scored after the final assessment of Management and Leadership criteria and will be scored according to the findings of financial and human resource management and other criteria dealing with adequate supply and effective management of resources (medication, consumables, equipment etc.). oral.description: The practice leaders facilitate communication between teams where relevant and between individual personnel members.
oral.guideline: ROOT "Teams" refer to all the groups of employees and practitioners involved in the practice, for example administration, cleaning and clinical personnel. Evidence of for example, minutes of personnel meetings, memos and communication tools can be provided as evidence. There must be evidence of at least quarterly personnel meetings.
oral.guidelines: ROOT "Teams" refer to all the groups of employees and practitioners involved in the practice, for example administration, cleaning and clinical personnel. Evidence of for example, minutes of personnel meetings, memos and communication tools can be provided as evidence. There must be evidence of at least quarterly personnel meetings.

oral.description: Agendas for meetings are prepared and shared in order to allow those attending to prepare for participation.
oral.guideline: An assessment of a sample of agendas of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and "general" compliance as the criterion should not be penalised on one or two exceptions.
oral.guidelines: An assessment of a sample of agendas of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and "general" compliance as the criterion should not be penalised on one or two exceptions.

oral.description: Minutes of meetings are taken and circulated to all relevant personnel.
oral.guideline: An assessment of a sampled minutes of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and "general" compliance as the criterion should not be penalised for one or two exceptions. The manner in which minutes are circulated should be assessed and should consist of documented evidence of the circulation and acknowledgment of receipt.
oral.guidelines: An assessment of a sampled minutes of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and "general" compliance as the criterion should not be penalised for one or two exceptions. The manner in which minutes are circulated should be assessed and should consist of documented evidence of the circulation and acknowledgment of receipt.

oral.description: There is a procedure to make sure that important matters resulting from management meetings are communicated to and acted upon by personnel.
oral.guideline: The existing "mechanism" should be assessed for effectiveness in conveying this information to personnel but surveyors must further note the importance of searching for evidence of "and acted upon". This generally requires that minutes should reflect the allocation of responsibilities for carrying out tasks and that these are reported on at subsequent meetings

oral.description: The practice plans and coordinates comprehensive care by establishing relations and maintaining contact with other relevant services and agencies, including both governmental and nongovernmental agencies.
oral.guideline: The purpose of this criterion is to ensure that practitioners are aware of members of the multidisciplinary team in both the private and government sector and engage them when required to ensure that the patient receives the best possible care. Documented evidence of such networking (for example, minutes of the meetings or email correspondence) should be made available for assessment.
oral.guidelines: The purpose of this criterion is to ensure that practitioners are aware of members of the multidisciplinary team in both the private and government sector and engage them when required to ensure that the patient receives the best possible care. Documented evidence of such networking (for example, minutes of the meetings or email correspondence) should be made available for assessment.

oral.description: Information on services, hours of operation and processes for obtaining care is provided to services in the community who work in collaboration with the practice.
oral.guideline: This information needs to be provided for example, to community nursing teams, step down care, allied health professionals etc. Compliance can be demonstrated by, for example, email correspondence with the various services or by providing information pamphlets about the practice to these services
oral.guidelines: This information needs to be provided for example, to community nursing teams, step down care, allied health professionals etc. Compliance can be demonstrated by, for example, email correspondence with the various services or by providing information pamphlets about the practice to these services

oral.description: The practice determines that the receiving individual/organisation can meet the patient's continuing care needs and establishes arrangements to ensure continuity.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence is required.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence is required.

oral.description: Policies and procedures that guide and support the different services offered by the practice are implemented.
oral.guideline: The criterion will be scored at the end of the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC Surveyors must document examples of non-compliance. This criterion will automatically be scored NC if there is evidence of non-compliance with policies and procedures that affect patient and personnel safety.
oral.guidelines: The criterion will be scored at the end of the survey. If this criterion is scored PC or NC Surveyors must document examples of non-compliance. This criterion will automatically be scored NC if there is evidence of non-compliance with policies and procedures that affect patient and personnel safety.

oral.description: Policies and procedures are signed and dated by persons authorised to do so.
oral.guideline: The initial implementation date and the name, designation and signature of the person authorising the policy must be clearly legible on each policy and procedure.
oral.guidelines: The initial implementation date and the name, designation and signature of the person authorising the policy must be clearly legible on each policy and procedure.

oral.description: Policies and procedures are correctly compiled, indexed and filed.
oral.guideline: Title: Each policy should have a name or title for identification and reference Identifying number: This is to facilitate indexing, locating the policy when required and communicating about the policy accurately Policy statement: What is to be achieved and why Procedure: How the policy is to be achieved. This is a step-by-step description of what needs to be done (like a recipe – following each step should result in a predictable outcome irrespective of who follows the steps) Associated references: This is required when the policy is directly associated with specific laws, regulations or policies or corporate policies and procedures Dates: The date of the original policy, the date(s) of any revision(s) and the date of the next planned review Signature: This is the signature of the person identified and authorised to approve policies and procedures If policies and procedures are only available electronically, all personnel must be able to demonstrate how to access the documents.
oral.guidelines: Title: Each policy should have a name or title for identification and reference Identifying number: This is to facilitate indexing, locating the policy when required and communicating about the policy accurately Policy statement: What is to be achieved and why Procedure: How the policy is to be achieved. This is a step-by-step description of what needs to be done (like a recipe – following each step should result in a predictable outcome irrespective of who follows the steps) Associated references: This is required when the policy is directly associated with specific laws, regulations or policies or corporate policies and procedures Dates: The date of the original policy, the date(s) of any revision(s) and the date of the next planned review Signature: This is the signature of the person identified and authorised to approve policies and procedures If policies and procedures are only available electronically, all personnel must be able to demonstrate how to access the documents.

oral.description: Policies and procedures are reviewed according to practice policy and then dated and signed.
oral.guideline: Policies and procedures must be reviewed at least every 5 years and when changes in evidence-based practice requires earlier review.
oral.guidelines: Policies and procedures must be reviewed at least every 5 years and when changes in evidence-based practice requires earlier review.

oral.description: There is a process to ensure that personnel are familiar with relevant policies and procedures.
oral.guideline: Compliance with this criterion can be achieved by means of, for example, signature sheets attached to each policy and procedure or by attendance registers at in-service training relating to policies and procedures.
oral.guidelines: Compliance with this criterion can be achieved by means of, for example, signature sheets attached to each policy and procedure or by attendance registers at in-service training relating to policies and procedures.

oral.description: There are documented processes for staffing the practice.
oral.guideline: Compliance with this criterion will require the practitioner to show documented evidence that patient load and patient acuity were studied and considered when planning the staffing for the practice. This must be evident for both the number of personnel required as well as their qualifications.
oral.guidelines: Compliance with this criterion will require the practitioner to show documented evidence that patient load and patient acuity were studied and considered when planning the staffing for the practice. This must be evident for both the number of personnel required as well as their qualifications.

oral.description: The desired education, qualifications, skills and knowledge are defined for personnel.
oral.guideline: These requirements must be discussed and agreed by all relevant personnel during the recruitment process and form part of the position description.
oral.guidelines: These requirements must be discussed and agreed by all relevant personnel during the recruitment process and form part of the position description.

oral.description: The processes include measures to improve personnel retention.
oral.guideline: Retention of personnel as opposed to the recruitment of new personnel is more cost effective and more likely to result in better patient care due to improved continuity of care and service provision and the accumulation of experience. It is therefore strategically important for retention to be considered as part of the recruitment process. As an additional benefit, measures taken to improve retention are likely to improve personnel satisfaction, and satisfied personnel provide better services. For a practice to be compliant with this criterion evidence must be provided of at least a personnel satisfaction survey.
oral.guidelines: Retention of personnel as opposed to the recruitment of new personnel is more cost effective and more likely to result in better patient care due to improved continuity of care and service provision and the accumulation of experience. It is therefore strategically important for retention to be considered as part of the recruitment process. As an additional benefit, measures taken to improve retention are likely to improve personnel satisfaction, and satisfied personnel provide better services. For a practice to be compliant with this criterion evidence must be provided of at least a personnel satisfaction survey.

oral.description: There is implementation of a reliable, documented process for evaluating and verifying the credentials of all health professionals.
oral.guideline: This can be achieved, for example, by having a standardised checklist of requirements that must be verified. This should include reference checks. The completed document can then be retained in the employee's personnel file. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of professional personnel.
oral.guidelines: This can be achieved, for example, by having a standardised checklist of requirements that must be verified. This should include reference checks. The completed document can then be retained in the employee's personnel file. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of professional personnel.

oral.description: Personnel files contain copies of qualifications and licences/registration from the relevant authority for all health professionals.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL  Copies of all the relevant original degrees/diplomas/certificates must be available, as well as evidence of registration with the relevant registration bodies. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files.
oral.guidelines: CRITICAL  Copies of all the relevant original degrees/diplomas/certificates must be available, as well as evidence of registration with the relevant registration bodies. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files.

oral.description: There is a system to track the annual registration of all health professionals.
oral.guideline: The system must reflect the date that each category of health professional's licence is due for renewal and document that the original, renewed licence has been seen.
oral.guidelines: The system must reflect the date that each category of health professional's licence is due for renewal and document that the original, renewed licence has been seen.

oral.description: All personnel with direct contact with the public have had a police check, a copy of which is kept in their personnel file.
oral.guideline: Police checks are repeated every 3 years or as appropriate. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel file.
oral.guidelines: Police checks are repeated every 3 years or as appropriate. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel file.

oral.description: There are documented processes for orientation of personnel to the practice.
oral.guideline: This includes general orientation and position-specific orientation as discussed in the standard intent above.
oral.guidelines: This includes general orientation and position-specific orientation as discussed in the standard intent above.

oral.description: The practice supports continuing education for its clinical personnel and maintains records of this in personnel files.
oral.guideline: This refers specifically to professional personnel and the requirements for continued registration with the relevant professional bodies, where applicable. Management must have a clear strategy for assisting professional personnel to maintain their continued registration.
oral.guidelines: This refers specifically to professional personnel and the requirements for continued registration with the relevant professional bodies, where applicable. Management must have a clear strategy for assisting professional personnel to maintain their continued registration.

oral.description: There is a development strategy for the practice that ensures that the practice manager and administrative personnel receive the training required to fulfil their responsibilities.
oral.guideline: This training could include billing, medical aid codes and triaging, (which is particularly important when administrative personnel are responsible for the initial assessment of patients in the waiting room.
oral.guidelines: This training could include billing, medical aid codes and triaging, (which is particularly important when administrative personnel are responsible for the initial assessment of patients in the waiting room.

oral.description: Personnel members are informed of opportunities to participate in advanced education, training, research, and other experiences.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of personnel or other training records. Personnel members may also be interviewed.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of personnel or other training records. Personnel members may also be interviewed.

oral.description: A designated person is responsible for the implementation and maintenance of a financial strategy.
oral.guideline: This requires that an individual in the practice has the officially assigned duties of overseeing and taking responsibility for all aspects of financial management. This may be the practice manager, a financial manager or a shared responsibility between practice manager and a financial officer.
oral.guidelines: This requires that an individual in the practice has the officially assigned duties of overseeing and taking responsibility for all aspects of financial management. This may be the practice manager, a financial manager or a shared responsibility between practice manager and a financial officer.

oral.description: The designated is suitably qualified and/or experienced in accounting and financial management.
oral.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.
oral.guidelines: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.

oral.description: The responsibilities of this person include ensuring that policies and procedures for all financial functions are implemented.
oral.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, policies and procedures must guide their functions.
oral.guidelines: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, policies and procedures must guide their functions.

oral.description: There is a current budget for the practice.
oral.guideline: For practices that comprise of more than one practitioner there should be an approved budget available in the management documentation. For practices that comprise of a solo practitioner there should be evidence of a practice budget. The budget must demonstrate allocation of resources. The budget will include, for example, salaries, rent, utility costs (water and electricity), consumable stock etc.
oral.guidelines: For practices that comprise of more than one practitioner there should be an approved budget available in the management documentation. For practices that comprise of a solo practitioner there should be evidence of a practice budget. The budget must demonstrate allocation of resources. The budget will include, for example, salaries, rent, utility costs (water and electricity), consumable stock etc.

oral.description: A report is produced at least annually, setting out the financial position to date.
oral.guideline: This criterion requires that at least the most recent financial report that is prepared annually for submission to the relevant authorities such as Botswana Unified Revenue Service (BURS) must be available during the survey.
oral.guidelines: This criterion requires that at least the most recent financial report that is prepared annually for submission to the relevant authorities such as Botswana Unified Revenue Service (BURS) must be available during the survey.

oral.description: There is a mechanism for establishing the reason for budget variation in either income or expenditure.
oral.guideline: Evidence should exist in the format in which financial statements are produced. Generally, these statements contain a column/section indicating the variance, i.e. under- or over-expenditure. Evidence showing what is done about this variance (especially over-expenditure), should be available, for example, in the minutes of relevant financial management meetings, personnel meetings or memos.
oral.guidelines: Evidence should exist in the format in which financial statements are produced. Evidence showing what is done about this variance (especially over-expenditure), should be available, for example, in the minutes of relevant financial management meetings, personnel meetings or memos.

oral.description: Capital investment proposals are subject to unanimous agreement among the partners or are agreed according to a voting system acceptable to all partners in the practice.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate agreement. This criterion will be scored "not applicable" in practices owned by a solo practitioner.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate agreement. This criterion will be scored "not applicable" in practices owned by a solo practitioner.

oral.description: The practice has documented ethical and legal policies and procedures for the financial management of the practice that are implemented.
oral.guideline: Examples of compliance with this criterion can be found in the policies and procedures for billing or referral to partners within the practice (for example partners in a poly clinic).
oral.guidelines: Examples of compliance with this criterion can be found in the policies and procedures for billing or referral to partners within the practice (for example partners in a poly clinic).

oral.description: Internal and external financial audit systems which meet audit requirements are maintained.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as Botswana Companies Act.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as Botswana Companies Act.

oral.description: Annual audited financial statements are produced within the required time frame.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as of the Botswana Companies Act.
oral.guidelines: CRITICAL Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as of the Botswana Companies Act.

oral.description: There is a capital asset register, which is routinely maintained.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence will be required.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence will be required.

oral.description: Assets are insured.
oral.guideline: This includes the practice assets and where applicable the physical structure.
oral.guidelines: This includes the practice assets and where applicable the physical structure.

oral.description: All health professionals provide evidence of professional indemnity insurance.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured during the personnel file audit.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured during the personnel file audit.

oral.description: An individual is designated to control the ordering, storage, distribution and control of equipment and supplies used in the practice.
oral.guideline: The store must be lockable and exclude any unauthorised entry.
oral.guidelines: The store must be lockable and exclude any unauthorised entry.

oral.description: Policies and procedures relating to all aspects of provisioning/supply chain management are implemented.
oral.guideline: As a minimum, the policies and procedures discussed in a) – d) in the standard intent above should be available, accessible to personnel and implementation should be monitored.
oral.guidelines: As a minimum, the policies and procedures discussed in a) – d) in the standard intent above should be available, accessible to personnel and implementation should be monitored.

oral.description: Secure storage facilities are available.
Prescription pads, letterheads, investigation request forms, administrative records and other official documents are accessible only to authorised persons.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be verified by observation and personnel interviews.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be verified by observation and personnel interviews.

oral.description: There are documented risk management processes for identifying all risks relating to practice processes and systems, personnel, patients, visitors to the practice and physical facilities.
oral.guideline: Compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence that the practice personnel have collaboratively identified risks within the practice. This document will be used to develop an action plan to eliminate or minimise risk. The risk management process should include all relevant aspects and services of the practice, for example patient, personnel and visitor related risks; financial, corporate and legal risks; physical facility, security and environmental risks; etc. This does not necessarily require a single integrated document, provided all components are dealt with in documented systems for the relevant operational processes/functions/sections. (NOTE: doing only monthly workplace inspections does not qualify for a compliance rating).
oral.guidelines: Compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence that the practice personnel have collaboratively identified risks within the practice. This document will be used to develop an action plan to eliminate or minimise risk. The risk management process should include all relevant aspects and services of the practice, for example patient, personnel and visitor related risks; financial, corporate and legal risks; physical facility, security and environmental risks; etc. This does not necessarily require a single integrated document, provided all components are dealt with in documented systems for the relevant operational processes/functions/sections. (NOTE: doing only monthly workplace inspections does not qualify for a compliance rating).

oral.description: The practice manager ensures the development and implementation of written policies and procedures for risk management processes and activities.
oral.guideline: These policies and procedures must define the routine risk management processes, for example, monthly inspections to monitor risks, and the actions to be taken when risks materialise as adverse events.
oral.guidelines: These policies and procedures must define the routine risk management processes, for example, monthly inspections to monitor risks, and the actions to be taken when risks materialise as adverse events.

oral.description: A nominated individual with relevant qualifications, skills and/or experience supervises the implementation of the risk management programme.
oral.guideline: In small practices, this will usually be the practice manager. Larger practices may have a designated risk manager.
oral.guidelines: In small practices, this will usually be the practice manager. Larger practices may have a designated risk manager.

oral.description: Ongoing in-service training of all personnel in risk management policies, procedures and principles is documented.
oral.guideline: A record must be kept of such training and must include all personnel.
oral.guidelines: A record must be kept of such training and must include all personnel.

oral.description: Risk management systems are reviewed whenever there are changes in practice systems and processes or physical facilities.
oral.guideline: This will include for example, during building renovations, changing from paper-based to electronic patient records or financial documentation, changing service providers etc.
oral.guidelines: This will include for example, during building renovations, changing from paper-based to electronic patient records or financial documentation, changing service providers etc.

oral.description: A nominated individual is responsible for infection control in the practice and practice personnel are aware of the nomination.
oral.guideline: It is this nominated individual’s responsibility to ensure that infection control policies and procedures are implemented and monitored.
oral.guidelines: It is this nominated individual’s responsibility to ensure that infection control policies and procedures are implemented and monitored.

oral.description: Written policies and procedures guide personnel in the implementation of the infection control programme.
oral.guideline: These policies and procedures must be available and accessible in the practice and their implementation monitored.
oral.guidelines: These policies and procedures must be available and accessible in the practice and their implementation monitored.

oral.description: Regular in-service training is given to all personnel in the field of infection control and is documented.
oral.guideline: The infection control education programme needs to include policies/guidelines as well as relevant issues as they are identified. All personnel should be included in information sharing and training at regular scheduled meetings or other fora. Documented evidence (for example, minutes of meetings/attendance records) must be provided.

oral.description: Infection control is on the agenda of all personnel meetings of the practice and discussion points are documented.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided.

oral.description: All patient and personnel areas of the practice are included in the documented infection control programme.
oral.guideline: This includes for example, all clinical areas, the waiting area, personnel and patient toilets, kitchen etc.
oral.guidelines: This includes for example, all clinical areas, the waiting area, personnel and patient toilets, kitchen etc.

oral.description: Hand washing and disinfecting facilities, including warm water, soap, paper towels or hand sanitisers are available in all relevant areas.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation.

oral.description: Personnel are constantly reminded of the importance of effective hand washing.
oral.guideline: For example, posters are displayed at basins, hand hygiene audits are regularly conducted etc.
oral.guidelines: For example, posters are displayed at basins, hand hygiene audits are regularly conducted etc.

oral.description: A nominated individual has been trained in and is responsible for sterilisation procedures within the practice and can describe the process in detail.
Relevant personnel members are immunised against Hepatitis B according to the policy.
oral.guideline: This requires the practice to formally identify the categories of personnel who are at risk (including cleaning personnel if they handle healthcare related waste) and to ensure that there is a system to track that their Hepatitis B immunisations remain up to date.
oral.guidelines: This requires the practice to formally identify the categories of personnel who are at risk (including cleaning personnel if they handle healthcare related waste) and to ensure that there is a system to track that their Hepatitis B immunisations remain up to date.

oral.description: There is a documented policy for the management of exposure to high-risk infections and needle stick injuries.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate that personnel are aware of the policy and have been trained on its content and implementation. Compliance will further be verified by personnel interviews.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate that personnel are aware of the policy and have been trained on its content and implementation. Compliance will further be verified by personnel interviews.

oral.description: Post exposure prophylaxis is available to personnel in accordance with national policy and includes the management of the patient from whom the needle was withdrawn.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL This includes exposure to high risk infections and needle stick injuries (for example body fluid splashes etc.) The national policy must be available and known to personnel.
oral.guidelines: CRITICAL This includes exposure to high risk infections and needle stick injuries (for example body fluid splashes etc.) The national policy must be available and known to personnel.

oral.description: There is a documented policy for the management of body fluid spills.
oral.guideline: The policy must include as a minimum: The correct cleaning procedure following a body fluid spill The correct procedure to follow when sluicing/washing contaminated linen
oral.guidelines: The policy must include as a minimum: The correct cleaning procedure following a body fluid spill The correct procedure to follow when sluicing/washing contaminated linen

oral.description: There is a documented policy for the triage of patients with potential communicable diseases.
oral.guideline: These patients are identified when requesting an appointment and appropriately triaged. If it is necessary for these patients to attend the practice, they are directed to a separate waiting area to prevent transmission of the infection to non-infected patients. Where there is no separate waiting area, the practitioner must attend to the patient immediately.
oral.guidelines: These patients are identified when requesting an appointment and appropriately triaged. If it is necessary for these patients to attend the practice, they are directed to a separate waiting area to prevent transmission of the infection to non-infected patients. Where there is no separate waiting area, the practitioner must attend to the patient immediately.

oral.description: The practice has a waste management policy that is consistent with country-specific laws and regulations and includes the safe handling, storing and disposing of all different types of waste.
oral.guideline: The policy must include all relevant aspects of waste management, for example, identification, colour coding, handling, storage, disposal of etc. with special reference to clinical/healthcare waste, personal protective equipment (PPE), the management of spills and the reporting and investigation of waste related incidents.
oral.guidelines: The policy must include all relevant aspects of waste management, for example, identification, colour coding, handling, storage, disposal of etc. with special reference to clinical/healthcare waste, personal protective equipment (PPE), the management of spills and the reporting and investigation of waste related incidents.

oral.description: The policy makes provision for the appropriate management of confidential waste.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL  Confidential waste includes all patient and personnel related documentation and practice-specific documentation for example, financial statements.
oral.guidelines: CRITICAL  Confidential waste includes all patient and personnel related documentation and practice-specific documentation for example, financial statements.

oral.description: Waste is segregated in accordance with policies, procedures, country-specific laws and regulations.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation.

oral.description: The colour of bag and type of container appropriate to the type of waste generated are available.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation.

oral.description: Waste is protected from theft, vandalism or scavenging by animals.
oral.guideline: Guideline statement:  Compliance will be measured by observation.
oral.guidelines: Guideline statement:  Compliance will be measured by observation.

oral.description: Waste is collected at appropriate times so that hazards are not caused.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation and by documented evidence that demonstrates when and by whom waste is collected.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation and by documented evidence that demonstrates when and by whom waste is collected.

oral.description: Security systems, including guards if required, provide for internal and external security.
oral.guideline: If the security system is outsourced (alarms/guards) the service contract must be available during the survey. As a minimum, a practice must be secured with burglar bars and security gates. Compliance will be further measured by observation of the security measures in place and whether they are appropriate for the individual setting.

oral.description: Sufficient light sources are available to provide adequate light (no dark areas) in all areas such as the entrance, waiting rooms, halls and offices.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation.

oral.description: There is effective control of access to clinical areas and store areas.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation.

oral.description: Alarm systems and signals are tested every month.
oral.guideline: This criterion will only be applicable to those practices that have alarm systems and signals (for example panic buttons) installed. Documented evidence of testing must be provided.
oral.guidelines: This criterion will only be applicable to those practices that have alarm systems and signals (for example panic buttons) installed. Documented evidence of testing must be provided.

oral.description: A mechanism known to the personnel is available for summoning the assistance of security/police/protection services in the case of an emergency.
oral.guideline: This can be achieved for example, by including the information in the orientation programme for new personnel and by displaying emergency numbers next to all telephones in the practice.
oral.guidelines: This can be achieved for example, by including the information in the orientation programme for new personnel and by displaying emergency numbers next to all telephones in the practice.

oral.description: Reasonable measures are taken to ensure the safety of lone workers.
oral.guideline: This criterion is only applicable where practitioners consult with patients after hours. Reasonable measures can include for example, the installation of panic buttons or notifying another person or security company that the practitioner is alone in the practice. Sufficient arrangements should be in place to ensure that a personnel member who is alone in the practice is safe. These arrangements must be outlined in a policy which all personnel should be aware of.
oral.guidelines: This criterion is only applicable where practitioners consult with patients after hours. Sufficient arrangements should be in place to ensure that a personnel member who is alone in the practice is safe. These arrangements must be outlined in a policy which all personnel should be aware of.

oral.description: There are structured systems and processes in place to ensure that all occupants of the practice's facilities are safe from fire or smoke.
oral.guideline: There are documented fire safety systems which include all the relevant aspects of fire safety, for example training, rehearsals, servicing and storage of equipment, escape route signage, storage and handling of flammable materials, etc.
oral.guidelines: There are documented fire safety systems which include all the relevant aspects of fire safety, for example training, rehearsals, servicing and storage of equipment, escape route signage, storage and handling of flammable materials, etc.

oral.description: Documented certification is available from the relevant authority to show that the facility complies with applicable laws and regulations in relation to fire safety (fire clearance certificate).
oral.guideline: CRITICAL  As per the intent statement, compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence of an official inspection to confirm that the building complies with national fire safety requirements. The practice manager must obtain a copy of the documentation from the landlord if the practice does not own the building.
oral.guidelines: CRITICAL  As per the intent statement, compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence of an official inspection to confirm that the building complies with national fire safety requirements. The practice manager must obtain a copy of the documentation from the landlord if the practice does not own the building.

oral.description: Firefighting equipment is regularly inspected and serviced at least annually, and the date of the service is recorded on the apparatus.
oral.guideline: Abatement systems as per country specific requirements will be reflected in the certification as required in 1.7.5.2. It is essential that the testing and servicing of all fire safety equipment is up to date, automatic abatement systems are regularly tested, fire and smoke detection systems are tested, and automatic abatement doors are not forced to remain open by means of wedging or putting objects against them. Documented evidence of inspection, testing and maintenance of fire safety equipment is required. This may include the fixing of service labels onto the equipment itself.
oral.guidelines: Abatement systems as per country specific requirements will be reflected in the certification as required in 1.7.5.2. It is essential that the testing and servicing of all fire safety equipment is up to date, automatic abatement systems are regularly tested, fire and smoke detection systems are tested, and automatic abatement doors are not forced to remain open by means of wedging or putting objects against them. Documented evidence of inspection, testing and maintenance of fire safety equipment is required. This may include the fixing of service labels onto the equipment itself.

oral.description: Flammable materials are clearly labelled and safely stored.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL Flammable materials are identified by the practice and stored in accordance with the national safety regulations. The storage precautions are applicable to all areas/services/departments where flammable materials are used. The appropriateness of the storage facility will be determined by the quantity and flashpoint of the materials stored. Bulk storage requirements and registration will be determined by country specific regulations.
oral.guidelines: CRITICAL Flammable materials are identified by the practice and stored in accordance with the national safety regulations. The storage precautions are applicable to all areas/services/departments where flammable materials are used. The appropriateness of the storage facility will be determined by the quantity and flashpoint of the materials stored. Bulk storage requirements and registration will be determined by country specific regulations.

oral.description: Sufficient electrical socket outlets are provided in all areas to avoid overloading of individual outlets and to minimise fire risks.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation.

oral.description: Easily recognised and understood signs prohibiting smoking are displayed in areas where flammable materials and combustible gases are stored.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be assessed during the assessment of the areas where such materials are stored.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be assessed during the assessment of the areas where such materials are stored.

oral.description: A floor plan showing the location of firefighting equipment, electrical distribution board, evacuation routes and emergency exits is displayed.
oral.guideline: The display of such plans throughout the practice will be observed during the visit to each area.
oral.guidelines: The display of such plans throughout the practice will be observed during the visit to each area.

oral.description: Annual personnel training in fire prevention and evacuation procedures is documented.
oral.guideline: A fire safety evacuation plan must be developed by the practice. Personnel training must be provided, and evacuation exercises held annually. Documented evidence that the plan is physically rehearsed will be required.
oral.guidelines: A fire safety evacuation plan must be developed by the practice. Personnel training must be provided, and evacuation exercises held annually. Documented evidence that the plan is physically rehearsed will be required.

oral.description: A mechanism known to personnel is available for summoning the fire service.
oral.guideline: This can be achieved for example, by including the information in the orientation programme for new personnel and by displaying emergency numbers next to all telephones in the practice.
oral.guidelines: This can be achieved for example, by including the information in the orientation programme for new personnel and by displaying emergency numbers next to all telephones in the practice.

oral.description: There is a written plan to deal with emergencies (including bomb threats, flooding, natural disasters, failure of water and electrical supplies).
oral.guideline: The documented plan is up to date and is available in all areas of the practice.
oral.guidelines: The documented plan is up to date and is available in all areas of the practice.

oral.description: There are site and floor plans that depict the locations and layout of the main services (for example, water, sanitation, electricity supply).
oral.guideline: It is very important that all personnel know where to switch off the main services, for example, water, electricity and, where installed, oxygen supply. Compliance will be measured on documented evidence and personnel interviews.
oral.guidelines: It is very important that all personnel know where to switch off the main services, for example, water, electricity and, where installed, oxygen supply. Compliance will be measured on documented evidence and personnel interviews.

oral.description: Documented evidence is available to show that the personnel participate in a rehearsal of the emergency plan at least annually.
oral.guideline: The emphasis of this rehearsal must be on the emergency situations mentioned in the standard intent. Documented evidence must be provided.
oral.guidelines: The emphasis of this rehearsal must be on the emergency situations mentioned in the standard intent. Documented evidence must be provided.

oral.description: First aid kits and materials for healthcare workers are available.
oral.guideline: Botswana specific occupational health laws and regulations should dictate the contents of the first aid kits. Please note that doctors may stock the required content as part of their regular stock. Other healthcare service providers will need to have a dedicated first aid kit. Compliance will be measured on the availability of the required stock.

oral.description: There is a documented policy for collection, collation, validation and distribution of data which is implemented.
oral.guideline: The policy must define those permitted access to each category of data and information.
oral.guidelines: The policy must define those permitted access to each category of data and information.

oral.description: Clinical, managerial and administrative personnel participate in developing and implementing an information system to support patient care and practice management.
oral.guideline: This does not have to be a single system, nor does it have to be electronic. Paper-based information system/s are also acceptable. The information required by the criteria must encompass at least information relating to individual patients, care provided, results of care and the practice’s own performance.
oral.guidelines: This does not have to be a single system, nor does it have to be electronic. Paper-based information system/s are also acceptable. oral.description: Clinical and managerial data and information are integrated as needed to support decision-making.
Required technology and other resources support the implementation of the data and information management system.
oral.guideline: Resource requirements may differ vastly between paper-based and computerised systems and the assessment of compliance needs to take these factors into account.
oral.guidelines: Resource requirements may differ vastly between paper-based and computerised systems and the assessment of compliance needs to take these factors into account.

oral.description: Security and confidentiality of data and information is maintained.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL Security relates to access control in terms of passwords, back up processes as well as archiving of paper-based records. Up to date antivirus/antimalware software is in place. Confidentiality aspects need to be documented as part of the information management processes for the various types of information, whether it be financial data, personal information on personnel, patient information, etc. Where applicable, country-specific legal requirements need to be considered.

oral.description: Documented procedures which outline the processes to provide required information to individuals and agencies outside the practice when required by laws or regulations are implemented.
oral.guideline: CRITICAL  Documented evidence of reporting is required. This includes notifiable disease reporting, other statistics required according to country-specific legislation and information provided to healthcare funders.
oral.guidelines: CRITICAL  Documented evidence of reporting is required. This includes notifiable disease reporting, other statistics required according to country-specific legislation and information provided to healthcare funders.

oral.description: The practice manager or delegated person checks the integrity of data leaving the facility for completeness, correctness and consistency.
oral.guideline: The integrity of data refers to the validation of raw data to ensure accuracy of information. Evidence of quality checks of the data before it leaves the practice and action taken when deficiencies in data quality and timeframes of reporting are identified. This includes for example, ICD 10 codes supplied to healthcare funders, national statistics etc.

This includes for example, ICD 10 codes supplied to healthcare funders, national statistics etc.

oral.description: Data is aggregated, analysed and transformed into useful, relevant information for monitoring and improving the service.
oral.guideline: The information generated by data collection tools, for example infection control audit tools, patient satisfaction surveys etc. must be analysed. This criterion will be scored non-compliant where there is only raw data available.
oral.guidelines: The information generated by data collection tools, for example infection control audit tools, patient satisfaction surveys etc. must be analysed. This criterion will be scored non-compliant where there is only raw data available.

oral.description: The frequency of data collection and analysis is appropriate to the process under study.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured on documented evidence that the frequency of data collection is established for each process under study.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured on documented evidence that the frequency of data collection is established for each process under study.

oral.description: Statistical tools and techniques are used in the analysis process when suitable.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence will be required for compliance.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence will be required for compliance.

oral.description: Information relating to the quality of the services delivered by the practice is made available to the patients of the practice and other relevant parties.
oral.guideline: This can be achieved by displaying the results in the waiting area, on personnel notice boards, on the practice website etc. The method of information sharing and the parties with whom it is shared will depend on the nature of the information.
oral.guidelines: This can be achieved by displaying the results in the waiting area, on personnel notice boards, on the practice website etc. The method of information sharing and the parties with whom it is shared will depend on the nature of the information.

oral.description: A designated individual oversees the information and quality management and improvement processes.
oral.guideline: This requires that an individual has the assigned duty of overseeing and taking responsibility for all aspects of information and quality management and improvement processes. The training and experience required will depend on the level of care provided by the practice and the complexity of the information systems for which the individual is responsible. In some practices this may be the practice manager or the solo practitioner, in other practices a quality manager may be appointed.

oral.description: All practice personnel are informed about the function of the information and quality management individual.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be measured during personnel interviews.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be measured during personnel interviews.

oral.description: There are formal systems and processes for quality management and improvement.
oral.guideline: This includes all aspects of the practice. Such processes may exist in different formats for example, a detailed single document, different documented systems or electronic modules. Whatever format exists, the intention is that the “processes” should be comprehensive and reflect all components of quality management and improvement in the practice.
oral.guidelines: This includes all aspects of the practice. Such processes may exist in different formats for example, a detailed single document, different documented systems or electronic modules. oral.description: Personnel are trained in the implementation of quality management processes.
oral.guideline: Evidence of compliance could exist in the form of attendance registers at such training sessions, information sharing meetings, availability of training manuals, policies and procedures etc.
oral.guidelines: Evidence of compliance could exist in the form of attendance registers at such training sessions, information sharing meetings, availability of training manuals, policies and procedures etc.

oral.description: The practice manager provides technology, support and resources (including time) for the assessment and improvement of the practice’s management, clinical and support processes and this is reflected in the strategic plan/business plan for the practice.
The leaders set priorities for improvement activities based on high risk, high cost and/or high volume or problem-prone areas.
oral.guideline: As part of the practice's quality management and improvement framework, the leaders should play an active role in collaborating with teams and individuals in the practice to decide which activities to include in the formal monitoring for quality improvement purposes.
oral.guidelines: As part of the practice's quality management and improvement framework, the leaders should play an active role in collaborating with teams and individuals in the practice to decide which activities to include in the formal monitoring for quality improvement purposes.

oral.description: Each function within the practice implements relevant quality improvement activities.
oral.guideline: The practice quality management and improvement framework needs to guide personnel on how to establish quality improvement processes and initiatives with regard to the development of relevant standards for their service, for example, administration, housekeeping, clinical care etc., the development of measurable indicators for monitoring purposes, and the implementation of remedial actions.
oral.guidelines: The practice quality management and improvement framework needs to guide personnel on how to establish quality improvement processes and initiatives with regard to the development of relevant standards for their service, for example, administration, housekeeping, clinical care etc., the development of measurable indicators for monitoring purposes, and the implementation of remedial actions.

oral.description: The objectives, scope, implementation and effectiveness of the activities to assess and improve quality are evaluated regularly and revised as necessary.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate that the quality management and improvement programme is evaluated and revised as necessary.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate that the quality management and improvement programme is evaluated and revised as necessary.

oral.description: Targets (goals) are set for the desired levels of patient care and practice management.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence is required.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence is required.

oral.description: The practice collects data relevant to each identified indicator in the quality improvement programme for the monitoring and improvement of patient care and practice management.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence is required.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence is required.

oral.description: As part of clinical monitoring, structured clinical audits are done to monitor the implementation of clinical guidelines.
oral.guideline: Clinical audits should measure actual performance against current best practice identified in guidelines compiled from evidence-based practices. Indicators of performance should be selected from these guidelines. Measurement of these indicators will provide information on current performance and evidence of change over time following the implementation of quality improvement plans.

oral.description: Information from the findings of quality assessment and improvement activities is used to detect trends, patterns and opportunities to improve or prevent potential problems.
oral.guideline: This requires evidence that areas for improvement are identified from all the data collected during practice activities.
oral.guidelines: This requires evidence that areas for improvement are identified from all the data collected during practice activities.

oral.description: When appropriate, an improvement plan is developed in collaboration with all relevant team members and an implementation process and acceptable timeframe is agreed to by the team and implemented.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided.

oral.description: A time for repeat data collection and analysis is agreed and completed and the results discussed by the relevant team members.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided.

oral.description: The practice holds regular meetings to discuss significant clinical issues.
oral.guideline: Evidence of significant clinical issues can be determined from the incident management processes and during patient record audits and personnel interviews. Minutes of meetings and attendance records must be provided as evidence of compliance with this criterion.
oral.guidelines: Evidence of significant clinical issues can be determined from the incident management processes and during patient record audits and personnel interviews. Minutes of meetings and attendance records must be provided as evidence of compliance with this criterion.

oral.description: Information from a validated patient/family satisfaction audit tool is used to improve the quality of service delivery.
oral.guideline: This requires that the practice does more than just provide information about the complaints process. Formal patient/family satisfaction surveys must be conducted regularly using an audit tool that is specifically designed to elicit information about the practice and the services that it offers. The tools used to measure patient feedback need to be rigorous and include all areas of the practice (for example waiting times, attitude of personnel, cleanliness of toilets, communication with clinical personnel, education etc.) to ensure the integrity of data subsequently used by practices for quality improvement purposes. The practice must decide on the method, frequency and number of patients to be surveyed but compliance with the criterion requires that it is representative of the size of the practice and the services offered. The practice needs to further provide documented evidence that the information from the survey was used to improve the quality of service delivery.

oral.description: A documented procedure for the monitoring of near misses/incidents/adverse (sentinel) events is available, which includes the documentation of interventions, responses and corrective actions taken to recorded incidents.
Formal significant event analyses are undertaken when necessary.
oral.guideline: The practice manager/risk manager must identify those events where formal event analyses must be conducted as required by law, regulation and practice policy. All events that meet the definition must be assessed by performing a credible root cause analysis. When the root cause analysis reveals that systems improvement or other actions can prevent or reduce the risk of such events recurring, appropriate action must be taken to achieve this risk reduction.

oral.description: Notes are kept regarding the data analysis and actions arising from formal incident reviews.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence will be required for compliance.

oral.description: All changes to policy and procedure that are identified as a result of these formal incident reviews are documented and included in new policies or incorporated into existing policies.

oral.description: Patient records are reviewed regularly, and results analysed as part of the quality improvement process.
oral.guideline: Documented evidence of such audits must be provided. Evaluation of results and remedial action taken must be documented as well as maintenance of achieved improvements over time.
oral.guidelines: Documented evidence of such audits must be provided. Evaluation of results and remedial action taken must be documented as well as maintenance of achieved improvements over time.

oral.description: The review uses a representative sample.
oral.guideline: A representative sample is a sufficiently large sample which includes records for all practitioners providing patient care and all types of care provided. The review should be conducted by clinical professionals authorised to make entries in the patient record.
oral.guidelines: A representative sample is a sufficiently large sample which includes records for all practitioners providing patient care and all types of care provided. The review should be conducted by clinical professionals authorised to make entries in the patient record.

oral.description: Records comply with professionally acceptable norms (including legal requirements where applicable) relating to signature, use of abbreviations and legibility.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

oral.description: Standardised diagnosis and procedure codes are used.
oral.guideline: Compliance will be assessed by comparing the codes used in the practice with country-specific directives. The responsible department should therefore have both the directives and the practice’s coding system available for comparison.
oral.guidelines: Compliance will be assessed by comparing the codes used in the practice with country-specific directives. The responsible department should therefore have both the directives and the practice’s coding system available for comparison.

oral.description: Symbols and definitions are standardised.
oral.guideline: The use of abbreviations provides an opportunity for miscommunication and confusion which can result in patient harm. Best practice is therefore not to use them at all. However, where they are permitted, their use must be standardised, and all relevant personnel must be made aware of the meaning of each abbreviation.

The patient's need for privacy is protected during all examinations, procedures and treatments.
oral.guideline: This must be evident in all patient care areas.
oral.guidelines: This must be evident in all patient care areas.

oral.description: Patient privacy is protected when providing personal information.
oral.guideline: This must be evident in all patient care areas including administrative areas and waiting areas. Patient privacy should be safeguarded at all times, including when providing personal information to nonclinical personnel. It is not always necessary to provide privacy by means of physical barriers such as walls and doors. Distance between waiting patients and those speaking with practice personnel can also provide the required privacy when less sensitive information is being exchanged. For example, a line can be drawn on the floor some distance away from the registration desk or pharmacy counter. Patients can be requested to wait behind the line and approach the counter only when providing their details or receiving their medication. Background noise, for example, music or patient education videos, can help to prevent conversations with patients being overheard.

oral.description: Policies and procedures to prevent the loss or misuse of patient information are implemented.
oral.guideline: This criterion measures more than just the information contained in the patient record. The confidentiality agreement that is signed by personnel must include the protection of all patient related information including for example, discussing patient details with anyone not involved in the patient’s care, comments on social media etc. Compliance will be measured by evidence of a signed confidentiality agreement and by examining the incident management system in the practice to establish if any complaints or incidents have been reported involving a breach of patient confidentiality.

oral.description: Documented consent is obtained from patients for health information to be provided to a third party.
oral.guideline: Evidence of compliance with this criterion may be found in different documents. For example, patients may give consent for information to be shared with health funders as part of their membership registration. Consent is required whenever health information is shared for example with employers, family members or carers, lawyers and insurance companies.

oral.description: A nominated individual within the practice is responsible for managing the complaints and ensuring that the complaints policy is implemented.
oral.guideline: This should be a senior person in the practice and is usually the solo practitioner or the practice manager.

oral.description: A nominated individual within the practice is responsible to oversee the investigation of and response to the complaint.
oral.guideline: Complainants must be kept informed of the progress of the investigation into the complaint. If there is a legitimate delay, they should be informed of the reason for the delay and provided with regular updates as to the progress of the management of their complaint. Documented evidence is required.
